name: Epoxies
body: The workhorse of resinous flooring: durable, chemical-resistant, and ideal for warehouses, manufacturing plants, and commercial garages. Self-leveling formulations create a smooth monolithic surface, while broadcast systems add slip resistance and visual depth.

name: Polyaspartic / Polyurea
body: Engineered for projects that can't afford downtime, these topcoats cure in hours instead of days and can be installed in temperature extremes where epoxies struggle. UV-stable and abrasion-resistant, they're a go-to finish for showrooms, garages, and any facility needing a fast return to service.

name: Polyurethane
body: A tougher, more flexible topcoat built to absorb impact and resist heavy mechanical wear in industrial environments. Ideal for high-traffic production floors, loading docks, and any surface that takes a beating from forklifts, pallets, and dropped tools.

name: Urethane Concrete
body: The standard for breweries, dairies, commercial kitchens, and food processing plants where floors face boiling water, freezing temps, and aggressive washdowns. Withstands extreme thermal cycling and organic acids that would destroy a standard epoxy system.

name: Patching / Joint
body: Foundational substrate prep used to repair spalled concrete, fill control joints, and create a sound base before a coating system goes down. Skipping this step is the fastest way to a failed floor, so we never do.

name: Chemical Resistant
body: Engineered for aggressive chemical exposure: secondary containment, battery rooms, chemical processing, and pharmaceutical manufacturing. These systems resist acids, caustics, and solvents that would etch or strip standard coatings within months.

name: Decorative
body: Where performance meets aesthetics, used in showrooms, retail spaces, hospitality interiors, and high-end residential garages. Flake and quartz systems add slip resistance and hide wear, while metallic finishes deliver a one-of-a-kind designer floor with industrial-grade durability underneath.
